18	// ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime carries the non-serializable plumbing and
19	// per-invocation runtime intent for an execute-bead loop run. Durable knobs
20	// (assignee, retry caps, harness/model, tier bounds, etc.) live on

28		// Claim. It is expected to call upstream ResolveRoute against the
29		// loop's resolved (harness, model) and return whatever typed routing
30		// error the upstream surfaced — notably agent.ErrHarnessModelIncompatible
31		// when the harness allow-list rejects the model. Any non-nil error
32		// causes the loop to exit immediately without claiming the bead, with
33		// a worker-level execution_failed record naming the rejected pair.
34		// DDx does NOT duplicate the upstream allow-list; this gate only
35		// consumes the typed-incompatibility surface.
36		RoutePreflight func(ctx context.Context, harness, model string) error
37		Once           bool

46	// DefaultReviewMaxRetries is the number of reviewer attempts allowed per
47	// committed result_rev before the loop emits a terminal
48	// `review-manual-required` event and stops re-executing primary. FEAT-022 §14.
49	const DefaultReviewMaxRetries = 3
50	
51	// MaxLoopCooldown is the absolute upper bound the execute-loop will set for
52	// any execute-loop-retry-after value. Year-scale parks effectively mean
53	// "never retry" and that should be a deliberate operator choice via
54	// `ddx bead update --set execute-loop-retry-after=...`, not an automatic
55	// loop decision. Beyond this cap, the loop refuses to lengthen the cooldown
56	// further; an operator extending it manually still works.
57	const MaxLoopCooldown = 24 * time.Hour
58	
59	// LandConflictCooldown is the short cooldown applied when all conflict-recovery
60	// paths fail (3-way ort auto-merge + optional focused-resolve agent) and the
61	// bead is parked as land_conflict_unresolvable. Much shorter than
62	// MaxLoopCooldown because land conflicts often resolve quickly as sibling beads
63	// advance the base branch.
64	const LandConflictCooldown = 15 * time.Minute
65	
66	// StoreErrorCooldown is the short cooldown applied when a Store.* operation in
67	// the outcome-handling block returns a transient error. It prevents an
68	// immediate re-queue of the affected bead while allowing the loop to continue
69	// to the next candidate. Store errors during outcome handling are non-fatal:
70	// they are logged and emitted as loop-error events but do not terminate the
71	// worker.
72	const StoreErrorCooldown = 5 * time.Minute
73	
74	// handleOutcomeStoreError is called when a Store.* operation in the
75	// post-Execute outcome block returns a non-nil error. It logs the error,
76	// appends a kind:loop-error event to the bead (best-effort), sets a short
77	// cooldown so the bead is not immediately re-queued, increments
78	// result.Failures, and returns true — signalling the caller to continue to
79	// the next loop iteration rather than returning.
80	//
81	// If ctx is already cancelled, it returns false so the caller can propagate
82	// the cancellation immediately.
83	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) handleOutcomeStoreError(ctx context.Context, beadID, operation string, storeErr error, assignee string, result *ExecuteBeadLoopResult, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime, now func() time.Time) bool {
84		if ctx.Err() != nil {
85			return false
86		}
87		if runtime.Log != nil {
88			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "outcome store error (%s %s): %v (continuing)\n", operation, beadID, storeErr)
89		}
90		_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
91			Kind:      "loop-error",
92			Summary:   operation + " failed",
93			Body:      storeErr.Error(),
94			Actor:     assignee,
95			Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
96			CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
97		})
98		cooldownUntil := now().UTC().Add(StoreErrorCooldown)
99		_ = w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(beadID, cooldownUntil, "loop-error", operation+": "+storeErr.Error())
100		result.Failures++
101		result.LastFailureStatus = "loop-error"
102		return true
103	}
104	
105	// capLoopCooldown clamps a loop-set cooldown duration to MaxLoopCooldown.
106	// The loop uses this for every SetExecutionCooldown call so no automatic
107	// path can silently park a bead for a year.
108	func capLoopCooldown(d time.Duration) time.Duration {
109		if d <= 0 {
110			return 0
111		}
112		if d > MaxLoopCooldown {
113			return MaxLoopCooldown
114		}
115		return d
116	}

349	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) Run(ctx context.Context, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) (*ExecuteBeadLoopResult, error) {
350		if w.Store == nil {
351			return nil, fmt.Errorf("execute-bead loop: store is required")
352		}
353		if w.Executor == nil {
354			return nil, fmt.Errorf("execute-bead loop: executor is required")
355		}
356	
357		now := w.Now
358		if now == nil {
359			now = time.Now
360		}
361		assignee := rcfg.Assignee()
362		if assignee == "" {
363			assignee = "ddx"
364		}
365		noProgressCooldown := rcfg.NoProgressCooldown()
366		if noProgressCooldown <= 0 {
367			noProgressCooldown = 6 * time.Hour
368		}
369		maxNoChangesBeforeClose := rcfg.MaxNoChangesBeforeClose()
370		if maxNoChangesBeforeClose <= 0 {
371			maxNoChangesBeforeClose = 3
372		}
373		heartbeatInterval := rcfg.HeartbeatInterval()
374		if heartbeatInterval <= 0 {
375			heartbeatInterval = bead.HeartbeatInterval
376		}
377		harness := rcfg.Harness()
378		model := rcfg.Model()
379		profile := rcfg.Profile()
380	
381		result := &ExecuteBeadLoopResult{}
382		attempted := make(map[string]struct{})
383		// triagedWarned suppresses the one-time boot warning when ComplexityGate is
384		// nil. One warning per Run() invocation is enough; the bool resets on the
385		// next worker boot.
386		triagedWarned := false
387		// hookFailed tracks beads whose pre-claim hook failed on first presentation
388		// in this run. A bead in hookFailed but not attempted gets one retry: on the
389		// second hook failure it moves to attempted so nextCandidate will skip it and
390		// the loop can exit gracefully. This prevents infinite spinning when the hook
391		// always fails while still allowing transient hook failures to be retried.
392		hookFailed := make(map[string]struct{})
393	
394		emit := func(eventType string, data map[string]any) {
395			writeLoopEvent(runtime.EventSink, runtime.SessionID, eventType, data, now().UTC())
396		}
397	
398		emit("loop.start", map[string]any{
399			"worker_id":    runtime.WorkerID,
400			"project_root": runtime.ProjectRoot,
401			"harness":      harness,
402			"model":        model,
403			"session_id":   runtime.SessionID,
404			"assignee":     assignee,
405			"once":         runtime.Once,
406		})
407		defer func() {
408			emit("loop.end", map[string]any{
409				"attempts":            result.Attempts,
410				"successes":           result.Successes,
411				"failures":            result.Failures,
412				"last_failure_status": result.LastFailureStatus,
413			})
414		}()
415	
416		for {
417			// Respect context cancellation between iterations. Without this check,
418			// a Stop() request (which cancels ctx) would only take effect during
419			// the idle poll sleep — the loop would happily claim the next ready
420			// bead as soon as the current Execute returned, ignoring the cancel.
421			if err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {
422				return result, err
423			}
424	
425			candidate, ok, err := w.nextCandidate(attempted, runtime.LabelFilter)
426			if err != nil {
427				return result, err
428			}
429			if !ok {
430				if result.Attempts == 0 {
431					result.NoReadyWork = true
432					if diag, ok := w.Store.(readyDiagnoser); ok {
433						if breakdown, bErr := diag.ReadyExecutionBreakdown(); bErr == nil {
434							result.NoReadyWorkDetail = NoReadyWorkBreakdown{
435								SkippedEpics:       breakdown.SkippedEpics,
436								SkippedOnCooldown:  breakdown.SkippedOnCooldown,
437								SkippedNotEligible: breakdown.SkippedNotEligible,
438								SkippedSuperseded:  breakdown.SkippedSuperseded,
439								NextRetryAfter:     breakdown.NextRetryAfter,
440							}
441						}
442					}
443				}
444				if runtime.PollInterval <= 0 {
445					return result, nil
446				}
447				if err := sleepWithContext(ctx, runtime.PollInterval); err != nil {
448					return result, err
449				}
450				continue
451			}
452	
453			// Pre-claim hook: fetch origin + verify ancestry before claiming.
454			// On error the bead is skipped for this iteration; the loop
455			// continues (ctx is not cancelled). The bead is NOT added to
456			// attempted on the FIRST failure so a transient hook failure (e.g.
457			// diverged branch) allows a retry on the next iteration. On a
458			// SECOND failure for the same bead in the same run it is moved into
459			// attempted so nextCandidate will skip it, preventing an infinite
460			// loop when the hook always fails.
461			if runtime.PreClaimHook != nil {
462				if hookErr := runtime.PreClaimHook(ctx); hookErr != nil {
463					if runtime.Log != nil {
464						_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "pre-claim hook: %v (skipping %s)\n", hookErr, candidate.ID)
465					}
466					emit("preclaim.skipped", map[string]any{
467						"bead_id": candidate.ID,
468						"reason":  hookErr.Error(),
469					})
470					if _, alreadyFailed := hookFailed[candidate.ID]; alreadyFailed {
471						// Second failure in this run: stop retrying this bead.
472						attempted[candidate.ID] = struct{}{}
473					} else {
474						hookFailed[candidate.ID] = struct{}{}
475					}
476					continue
477				}
478			}
479	
480			attempted[candidate.ID] = struct{}{}
481	
482			// Routing preflight gate (FEAT-006 D3, ddx-98e6e9ef): consult the
483			// upstream typed-incompatibility surface BEFORE claiming. If the
484			// configured (harness, model) cannot serve the bead, exit the loop
485			// with a worker-level failure record — no claim, no executor
486			// invocation, no tier-attempt event burn.
487			if runtime.RoutePreflight != nil {
488				if rerr := runtime.RoutePreflight(ctx, harness, model); rerr != nil {
489					detail := fmt.Sprintf("routing preflight rejected (harness=%s model=%s): %s",
490						harness, model, rerr.Error())
491					if runtime.Log != nil {
492						_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "routing preflight: %s (skipping %s, exiting loop)\n",
493							detail, candidate.ID)
494					}
495					emit("preflight.rejected", map[string]any{
496						"bead_id": candidate.ID,
497						"harness": harness,
498						"model":   model,
499						"reason":  rerr.Error(),
500					})
501					report := ExecuteBeadReport{
502						BeadID:  candidate.ID,
503						Status:  ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
504						Detail:  detail,
505						Harness: harness,
506						Model:   model,
507					}
508					result.Attempts++
509					result.Failures++
510					result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
511					result.Results = append(result.Results, report)
512					return result, nil
513				}
514			}
515	
516			// Complexity triage gate (ddx-5bf4ee7e): pre-Claim decomposition check.
517			// When the gate is nil, decomposition responsibility shifts to the
518			// LLM-driven path inside execute-bead (ddx-b790449b AC5): the agent
519			// is prompted to recognize coarse beads, file children, and exit
520			// early. The pre-dispatch warning is intentionally suppressed —
521			// it is no longer accurate now that decomposition lives downstream.
522			if w.ComplexityGate == nil {
523				_ = triagedWarned // retained for future re-introduction of a typed gate
524			} else {
525				shouldClaim, triageErr := w.ComplexityGate(ctx, candidate)
526				if triageErr != nil {
527					if runtime.Log != nil {
528						_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log,
529							"triage gate error for %s: %v (skipping)\n", candidate.ID, triageErr)
530					}
531					emit("triage.error", map[string]any{

15		cmd := &cobra.Command{
16			Use:   "work",
17			Short: "Drain the bead execution queue",
18			Long: `work drains the execution-ready bead queue. It is the FEAT-010 layer-3
19	queue drain: it iterates ddx try (layer 2) across ready beads until a stop
20	condition is met and owns retry-power policy between attempts.
21	
22	Unlike "ddx agent execute-loop", ddx work treats --harness, --provider, and
23	--model as opaque passthrough constraints forwarded to the agent unchanged.
24	DDx does not validate these values or branch on them; the agent owns routing
25	within the requested power bounds.
26	
27	Stop conditions (evaluated between attempts):
28	  drained     — no ready beads remain
29	  blocked     — every remaining bead has produced a terminal non-success outcome
30	  deferred    — configured budget exhausted
31	  no_progress — N consecutive attempts produced no commit (default N=3)
32	  signal      — SIGINT/SIGTERM received between attempts
33	
34	By default work submits to the running ddx server as a background worker and
35	returns immediately. Use --local to run inline in the current process.
36	`,
37			Example: `  # Drain the current execution-ready queue once and exit
38	  ddx work
39

41	  ddx work --local --once
